label: Prohibited AI practices (Art. 5) — applicable from 2 February 2025
note: Chapter II (Art. 5) applies 6 months after entry into force (1 August 2024). All prohibited AI systems must be withdrawn from market by this date. GPAI model obligations (Art. 53-56) also apply from this date.

label: GPAI model obligations (Art. 53-56) — applicable from 2 February 2025
note: Art. 53, 54, 55, and 56 (general-purpose AI model obligations, including systemic risk) apply 6 months after entry into force. Providers of existing GPAI models must comply from this date.

label: Notifying authorities, GPAI governance, Chapter VII — applicable from 2 August 2025
note: Chapter III Section 4 (notifying authorities and notified bodies), Chapter V (governance of GPAI), Chapter VII (national competent authorities and AI governance structures), Chapter XII (final provisions) apply 12 months after entry into force.

label: Full application of most provisions — 2 August 2026
note: Most provisions of the Regulation, including: Chapter III (high-risk AI system requirements and obligations — Art. 8-49), Chapter IV (transparency obligations — Art. 50), Chapter VI (AI governance — Art. 57-63), Chapter VIII (post-market monitoring — Art. 72-94), Chapter IX (codes of conduct — Art. 95), Chapter X (penalties — Art. 99-101). This is the primary compliance deadline for providers of Annex III high-risk AI systems.

label: Art. 6(1) high-risk AI in Annex I products and public authority Annex III deployers — 2 August 2027
note: Art. 6(1) and the corresponding high-risk AI system obligations apply 36 months after entry into force for: (a) AI systems that are safety components of Annex I products (Union harmonisation legislation) requiring 3rd-party conformity assessment; and (b) according to Art. 113, public authority deployers of Annex III systems. This later deadline allows time for alignment with existing product conformity assessment regimes.

label: Serious incident reporting — death: within 2 days
note: Where the serious incident involves the death of a person, the provider must report without undue delay and within 2 days of becoming aware.

label: Serious incident reporting — death or serious health damage: within 10 days
Serious incident reporting — other serious incidents: within 15 days
Log retention by deployers — minimum 6 months
note: Minimum retention period. Longer retention may be required by EU or national law applicable to the specific sector or use case.
